channel.title: Global Industrial & Manufacturing News (Mock Feed)
channel.description: Simulated RSS feed covering all event types for testing PRO SICHT AI.

item.title: Bosch to relocate steering column production from Germany to Hungary
item.description: Automotive supplier Bosch announced it is shifting its steering column production operations from its Stuttgart facility to a new site in Miskolc, Hungary, within the next 6 months to reduce operational costs.

item.title: Ford shuts down historic Saarlouis plant, lays off 3,000 workers
item.description: Ford Motor Company has officially confirmed the bankruptcy and closing of its vehicle assembly facility in Saarlouis, Germany. Production will halt entirely by the end of the year.

item.title: TSMC announces $2 Billion capacity increase at Arizona fab
item.description: Semiconductor giant TSMC is scaling up operations at its Phoenix, Arizona plant. The company plans to invest an additional $2 billion to expand the facility and add a new 3nm chip production line.

item.title: Tesla breaks ground on greenfield Gigafactory in Mexico
item.description: Tesla held a groundbreaking ceremony today for its brand new facility in Nuevo León, Mexico. This greenfield project will be the company's largest electric vehicle manufacturing hub in Latin America.

item.title: French Ministry of Defense issues tender for military drone procurement
item.description: The French government has released a new public bidding contract (tender offer) seeking suppliers for 500 tactical surveillance drones. Contract award is expected in Q3 2026.

item.title: Siemens CEO Roland Busch extends contract until 2030
item.description: Siemens AG supervisory board has agreed to extend the contract of CEO Roland Busch for another five years. The company reported steady quarterly earnings despite market volatility.
